frames: The agent stands upright, then begins to bend its right knee and lift its left foot slightly, while its left arm moves forward.
The agent continues to bend its right knee, shifting its weight, with its left leg extending backward and left arm forward.
The agent's right leg is deeply bent, supporting its weight, as the left leg lifts off the ground and swings back.
The agent is in a deep lunge on its right leg, with the left leg bent high behind it and arms in a running posture.
The agent straightens its right leg, bringing the left leg down to the ground, returning to an upright stance.
The agent stands momentarily with both feet on the ground, appearing stable and upright.
The agent then bends its left knee, lifting its right foot, and leans forward, initiating another step.
The agent's left leg buckles, and it falls forward onto its hands and knees, failing to maintain balance.
